original: British-Belgian teen Mack Rutherford (17) becomes the youngest person to fly solo around the world, landing in Sofia, Bulgaria, after a five-month journey across 52 countries
US adventurer Victor Vescovo becomes the first person to visit the deepest point of every ocean when he reaches Molloy Hole in the Arctic
Astronomers announce the discovery of an Earth-like planet named Proxima b orbiting the star Proxima Centauri
PhysicistStephen Hawkingpresents a new theory on black holes at a lecture at the KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm
Amelia Earhartbegins the first transcontinental non-stop flight by a woman in Los Angeles, California
American scientist Peter Schultz announces the possibility of Martian tornadoes
International Astronomical Union (IAU) redefines the term "planet," classifying Pluto as a dwarf planet
Naturalist John Henslow writes toCharles Darwinto offer him a place aboard the Admiralty surveying vessel HMS Beagle on a voyage to chart the South American coast
Kenny Baker, English actor (R2-D2 in Star Wars, Time Bandits), born in Birmingham, England (d. 2016)
Thomas Edisonpatents the motion picture camera
Air Transat Flight 236 runs out of fuel over the Atlantic Ocean due to a fuel leak and makes a successful emergency landing in the Azores, saving all 306 people on board
Having pitched into the 9th inning, Cleveland's Ray Caldwell is flattened by a bolt of lightning and goes on to record the final out for the Indians' 2-1 win over the Philadelphia A's

Believed until 2018 to be the date of the massive eruption of Mt. Vesuvius which buried the cities of Pompeii, Herculaneum, Oplontis and Stabiae, killing untold thousands, latest evidence suggests the eruption occurred after 17 October
Duke Kahanamoku, American swimmer, surfer (Olympic gold 100m freestyle 1912, 20; 4x200m freestyle relay 1920), born in Honolulu, Hawaii (d. 1968)
First potato chips are prepared by chef George Crum at Moon's Lake House near Saratoga Springs, New York (popular legend says he invents them, though earlier recipes exist)
Argon fluorohydride, the first Argon compound ever known, is discovered at the University of Helsinki by Finnish scientists
Americans Kerri Walsh and Misty May team up to win the women's beach volleyball gold at the Athens Olympics beating Brazilian pair Shelda Bede & Adriana Behar 21–17, 21–11
Yelena Isinbayeva of Russia vaults 4.91 m to break her own pole vault world record and wins the gold medal from teammate Svetlana Feofanova at the Athens Olympics
UN says Madagascar is on the brink of the world's first "climate change famine" with people suffering "catastrophic" levels of hunger after four years without rain
The printing of the Gutenberg Bible is completed
-127°F (-88°C) recorded in Vostok, Antarctica; world record low temperature at the time, surpassed in 1983
American Charles Chapman is the first Black person to swim the English Channel
Anna Lee Fisher [Tingle], American chemist, emergency physician and astronaut (STS 51-A), born in St Albans, New York

Tim White, American paleoanthropologist best known for leading the team which discovered Ardi, the type specimen of Ardipithecus ramidus, a 4.4 million-year-old likely human ancestor, born in Los Angeles County, California
British Professor Kevin Warwick is the first human to receive an RIFD (radio-frequency identification) microchip implant to interact with electronic systems
The star-studded American men's basketball team, led byLeBron James, Chris Paul, andKobe Bryant, wins the gold medal at the Beijing Olympics with a relatively narrow 118-107 victory over Spain

Samuel Wanjiru becomes the first Kenyan to win the men's Olympic marathon in Beijing, winning in an Olympic record time; silver medalist Jaouad Gharib of Morocco is also faster than the Olympic record, yet finishes 44 seconds behind
Kieren Perkins swims a world record in the 1500 m freestyle (14:41.66)
Kieren Perkins swims a world record in the 800 m freestyle (7:46.00)
Seattle Mariners slugger Cal Raleigh hits two home runs, including his 49th, breaking MLB season record for a catcher in 11-4 win over visiting Oakland A's
Bill Veeck's MLB St. Louis Browns hold "Grandstand Manager's Day" as coaches hold up placards for fans to vote on strategy options; Browns defeat visiting A's 5-3
First 200-meter freestyle swum in under 2 minutes by Don Schollander (1:58)
USSR launches Luna 11 into orbit around the Moon
Hurricane Andrew hits South Florida, making landfall at Elliott Key and later Homestead; 44 people die, and $25 billion in damage is recorded
Microsoft debuts Windows 95
Musical film "Rock 'n' Roll High School," directed by Allan Arkush and featuring theRamones, premieres in NYC and is released throughout the US 20 days later
Orson Scott Card, American sci-fi author (Hugo; Nebula; Ender's War), born in Richland, Washington
Rocky Johnson, Canadian pro wrestler (father of Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson; with Tony Atlas first black champions in WWE history), born in Amherst, Nova Scotia, Canada (d. 2020)
Franz Viehböck, Austrian 1st cosmonaut (Soyuz TM-13), born in Vienna, Austria
Salizhan Sharipov, Kyrgyzstani cosmonaut (STS-89), born in Uzgen, Kyrgyzstan
Ray McIntire, American chemical engineer (invented Styrofoam at Dow), born in Gardner, Kansas (d. 1996)
American inventor Cornelius Swarthout patents a stovetop waffle iron
Gordie HoweandJean Béliveauare inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame
Gordon Spence discovers 2^2976221 - 1 as the 36th known Mersenne prime
At 17, future England soccer striker Jimmy Greaves scores on his First Division debut for Chelsea against Tottenham Hotspur in a 1–1 draw at White Hart Lane
MLB Cleveland Indians aceBob Fellerreturns from serving in the US Navy and strikes out 12 batters
Rome is overrun by Visigoths underAlaric Ifor the first time in nearly 800 years, seen as the fall of the Western Roman Empire
Liberia adopts a national flag designed and hand-stitched by a committee of seven women chaired by Susannah Elizabeth Lewis; all of the women are born in the US
Workers start pouring concrete for the Panama Canal
HMS Beagle reaches Bahía Blanca, Argentina
Soyuz 21 returns to Earth
STS 51-I mission is scrubbed at T-5 minutes due to bad weather
Rock band "The Cars" plays a concert at Wollman Rink in NYC's Central Park
Ukraine declares independence from the Soviet Union
Edith Sampson is named the first African American U.S. delegate to the UN
Hiram L. Fong is sworn in as the first Chinese-American US Senator, while Daniel K. Inouye is sworn in as the first Japanese-American US Representative, both from Hawaii
After signing a one-day contract with the San Francisco 49ers,Jerry Riceofficially retires from the NFL
Daniel Gooch, English engineer, laid 1st successful transatlantic cables, born in Bedlington, Northumberland, England (d. 1889)
Albert Claude, Belgian biologist and physician (Nobel 1974), born in Longlier, Belgium (d. 1983)
Claude Hopkins, American jazz stride pianist, arranger, bandleader, and musical director (Josephine Baker), born in Alexandria, Virginia (d. 1984)
